name: Fairview Savings and Loan Association
description_short: Community bank in Fairview, Oklahoma offering checking, savings, loans, and traditional banking services with online and mobile access.
description_long: Fairview Savings and Loan Association is a community-based financial institution located in Fairview, Oklahoma, serving local customers since operating from their main branch at 301 N. Main Street. The institution operates as a traditional savings and loan association, providing core banking products and services to individuals and businesses in the area. They emphasize personal service and claim to 'work hard to earn your business and keep it.' The association maintains limited physical infrastructure with one main location and standard business hours. The bank offers a full range of traditional banking products including personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts, personal loans, and mortgage lending. They provide online banking with personal and business enrollment options, a mobile app for account access, debit cards, and a 24-hour ATM. Their service menu also includes bank by mail, cashier's checks, money orders, wire transfers, notary services, stop payments, and financial calculators to assist customers with planning. Fairview Savings distinguishes itself through community-focused service, including free notary services to customers and convenient after-hours deposit capabilities via night drop. They emphasize security and privacy, with explicit warnings about unsecured email communication and direction toward secured online banking channels for sensitive inquiries. The institution maintains traditional banking values with human staff, multiple notaries on hand, and personalized service rather than purely digital operations. As a small community bank, Fairview Savings operates with limited reach and appears focused on serving local Oklahoma residents rather than providing competitive rates or features versus larger regional or national institutions. The website provides minimal information about loan terms, interest rates, fees (beyond references to 'modest' or 'nominal' charges), or account requirements. Customers should contact them directly for specific product details and rate information.
pros: Free notary services provided by multiple staff notaries with only photo ID required
Night drop box available on west side for secure after-hours deposits and payments
Mobile banking app available for account access on the go
Convenient drive-thru lanes plus full-service lobby available
Same-day posting for deposits and loan payments received by mail
Wire transfer services available for same-day fund transfers to other institutions
24-hour ATM access at the main branch location
cons: Single branch location in Fairview, OK limits accessibility for non-local customers
Limited business hours (9 AM - 4 PM Monday-Friday) with no weekend lobby service
Website lacks transparency on interest rates, loan terms, and specific fee schedules
No information provided about FDIC insurance, account minimums, or product details
Small community bank unlikely to offer competitive rates or features versus regional banks
best_for: Residents and businesses in Fairview, Oklahoma seeking local banking relationships
Customers who prefer traditional in-person banking with human interaction
Small business owners needing basic business checking and lending services
People valuing free notary services and after-hours deposit convenience
diagnosis: Fairview Savings and Loan Association is best for individuals and small businesses in the Fairview, Oklahoma area who value personalized, local banking services and don't require extensive branch networks or digital-first banking. The main caveat is that as a single-branch community bank, it provides limited reach and accessibility for non-local customers, and the website provides insufficient transparency about rates, fees, and specific product terms to make informed comparisons with other institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
